name: Sherrod Brown
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Sherrod Brown
body:
|
---

| | Peter, It's only been a couple of weeks since the election. You may have seen news coverage confirming that, for the first time in my career, I am considering running for President. I am reading your comments about this on Facebook and Twitter, and in private messages. What an honor to be part of that conversation. Peter, there is a lot of work to do to help families across Ohio and this nation. I take that fight and that responsibility very seriously. Too many leaders in Washington don't understand the dignity of work. Our economic policies reward huge companies that ship jobs overseas and leave workers behind. CEOs rake in massive bonuses after taking away workers' rights to bargain for better working conditions and benefits. We have to continue fighting for _all Americans_ \-- regardless of your zip code, color of your skin, your religion, or whom you love. Whether you swipe a badge or punch a clock, rely on tips, or collect a salary. Our victory showed what happens when you fight for the people who make this country work -- and that is a blueprint for how Democrats can win in 2020. Over the coming weeks and months, Connie and I will be discussing with family and friends whether I should run for President. But one thing is already clear: _I wouldn 't be running for President alone._ Everything that we've accomplished has not been because of one person -- it's because of this movement. Our successes are the result of grassroots organizing. When people like you stand together and fight for our friends, family, neighbors, and communities, **we make change a reality.[That's why it's so important that I hear your opinion about 2020. If you can, let us know your thoughts now.](https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/page/m/302ffd74/4462dc26/8b53faa7/201f0ec6/2099514551/VEsH/ "https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/e181120m")** Thank you for being a member of the best grassroots team in the country. Onward, and with gratitude, Sherrod
---

|
---
[www.sherrodbrown.com](https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/page/m/302ffd74/4462dc26/8b53faa7/201f0ec5/2099514551/VEsE/ "https://www.sherrodbrown.com/")
| | Paid for by Friends of Sherrod Brown
---

name: Mary Mancini
subject: Join Us: Statewide Analyze, Organize, Mobilize Tour Kicks Off Thursday, Nov. 29
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE TENNESSEE DEMOCRATIC PARTY, DR
body:
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/rgc3USjzCKV87CVFvpLu9sBFI1UAr7WPFTRLpQ1y7xU=/v-OIO76E12RmOg6Ca2u1x4iPESRBuWx92YcpuajLpNY=)
#
Dear Peter,

**_" When we gather around our tables...to share the fruits of our blessings, let's remember what that means. Let's be grateful for what we have. Let's be mindful of those who have less. Let's appreciate those who hold a special place in our lives, and make sure that they know it. And let's think about those who can't spend the holiday with their loved ones -- especially the members of our military serving overseas."_**

Reflecting on this passage from a Barack Obama Thanksgiving address, the President invites Americans to not just give thanks for what we have, but to understand that not everyone -- in this country and so many others -- are as fortunate.

The Tennessee Democratic Party wishes you and your loved ones a happy and safe Thanksgiving. We're grateful for all the ways in which you care for your communities and fight for a better life for your neighbors, families, and friends. This Thanksgiving, we're especially thankful for the grassroots power of Democrats across the country leading us toward a better future.

[](https://act.myngp.com/el/rgc3USjzCKV87CVFvpLu9sBFI1UAr7WPFTRLpQ1y7xU=/wZolnHJkM1Mbe0LL9ZYvJrLt3pPczDiIO25SiImd110=?ref=page_internal)

Next week, we will begin a statewide tour. Each stop of the TNDP _ANALYZE, ORGANIZE, MOBILIZE_ Tour will be a public forum -- a great opportunity for you, as a member of the Tennessee Democratic Party, to continue the important work you've done over the past two years by participating in an open discussion about the 2018 election cycle and moving forward in 2019, 2020, and beyond.

We look forward to seeing you in the coming weeks. Have a joyous Thanksgiving weekend.

Mary and the TNDP Team
\--
Mary Mancini
Chair, Tennessee Democratic Party

* * *
**Analyze** •**Organize** •**Mobilize**
**Tour Schedule**

Thursday, November 29 - [Jackson, TN](https://act.myngp.com/el/rgc3USjzCKV87CVFvpLu9sBFI1UAr7WPFTRLpQ1y7xU=/AGzPiLuZnovuAcijsdGcwfk2-9YofSswkw4rESvHu6o=)

Saturday, December 1 - Shelby County (Details TBD)

Monday, December 3 - [Rutherford County](https://act.myngp.com/el/rgc3USjzCKV87CVFvpLu9sBFI1UAr7WPFTRLpQ1y7xU=/wsHhd82J7m_dHTpNhlLZvMdncmD2L1CQCDDlKbToFNg=)

Tuesday, December 5 - [Davidson County](https://act.myngp.com/el/rgc3USjzCKV87CVFvpLu9sBFI1UAr7WPFTRLpQ1y7xU=/OqOSb_53VQBtb4TkQKC0IHU3f05SFx1X5lz2_eYD91M=)

Thursday, December 6 - [Tri-Cities Area](https://act.myngp.com/el/rgc3USjzCKV87CVFvpLu9sBFI1UAr7WPFTRLpQ1y7xU=/wbPMpA45JGP5r8tJmDHqOZiyL8jc56r39qai9-4o_oY=)

Saturday, December 8 - [Knox County](https://act.myngp.com/el/rgc3USjzCKV87CVFvpLu9sBFI1UAr7WPFTRLpQ1y7xU=/B-YkCSMgWVqDQ-JviX5Q6qD2gxp9pIGeaqpNFaWqOXM=)

Saturday, December 8 - [Hamilton County](https://act.myngp.com/el/rgc3USjzCKV87CVFvpLu9sBFI1UAr7WPFTRLpQ1y7xU=/Hool29RkAkgpZmcSD-buBnQF3Ydf6b8mkzgCv6AK-K8=)

Monday, Dec 10 - [Putnam County](https://act.myngp.com/el/rgc3USjzCKV87CVFvpLu9sBFI1UAr7WPFTRLpQ1y7xU=/mR9mUYdC-vuREFRaZWCHmi6xt_JaTpInq3Aym838WAY=)

name: Dan Lipinski
subject: Happy Thanksgiving!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dan Lipinski for Congress Contributions or gifts to Dan Lipinski for Congress are not tax deductible
body:
| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/3289510/22846205/368741054?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83MDg4MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwOTAzNDZlMi1hZWVkLWU4MTEtODYwNy0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=qAUu_T7H9jnKUj9qf2qENZr3bCHHt1GYV7AiyBPmiwk=)
Happy Thanksgiving, Peter.

Every day I’m reminded of how grateful I am to represent the hardworking families of Illinois’ 3rd district, and how thankful I am for my own family too.

I’m grateful for all the support I’ve received from each and every one of you over the last year, and I hope to make you proud when I head back to Washington after the holidays.

Have a Happy Thanksgiving, and enjoy your time off with family and friends — and the turkey, of course!

Thanks,

Dan

| |

name: Sherrod Brown
subject: Damn right.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Sherrod Brown
body:
|
---

| | When you win a big election like we did, the press likes to pick apart how you did it. One news outlet said the reason I won was because I've spent my career _"obsessing, first and foremost, over the concerns of workers."_ I have one thing to say about that Peter: **You're damn right.** | | | [ADD YOUR NAME IF YOU'RE WITH ME](https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/page/m/302ffd75/4462dc26/8b68f552/201f0ea8/457840588/VEsH/ "https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/e181119z")
---
Too many leaders in Washington don't understand the dignity of work. Our economic policies reward huge companies that ship jobs overseas and leave workers behind, struggling to make ends meet. CEOs rake in huge bonuses after taking away workers' rights to bargain for better working conditions and benefits.
I'm going back to Washington to keep fighting for _all_ Americans -- regardless of their zip code, economic status, or color of their skin. **And as we move to implement policies that reward those who make this country work, I need your support.**
[**Add your name to pledge to continue our work, together, in fighting for American workers and their families. >>**](https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/page/m/302ffd75/4462dc26/8b68f552/201f0ea8/457840588/VEsE/ "https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/e181119z")
Ohio showed the country that by putting hardworking people first and honoring the dignity of work, we can carry a state Donald Trump won by nearly 10 points.
Thank you for standing with me for the past two years. Now, our work continues. I hope I can count on you to continue fighting for our priorities.
| | [COUNT ME IN](https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/page/m/302ffd75/4462dc26/8b68f552/201f0ea8/457840588/VEsF/ "https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/e181119z")
---
With gratitude,
Sherrod

|
---

name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: President Trump Pardons Both Peas and Carrots
body:
President Donald Trump has pardoned Peas, a 39-pound turkey that traveled all the way from South Dakota... | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=4ea68b7a44&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **President Trump Pardons Both Peas and Carrots**

“President Donald Trump has pardoned Peas, a 39-pound turkey that traveled all the way from South Dakota to be dubbed the 2018 National Thanksgiving Turkey,” Virginia Allen writes in _The Daily Signal_. “Americans this week were given the opportunity to vote on which turkey—Peas or buddy Carrots—should receive the formal presidential pardon, sparing him from becoming Thanksgiving dinner. The nation chose Peas, but Trump pardoned both.”

“This is a time for Americans to unite together in a spirit of love, understanding, unity, and joy, as one very proud American family,” the President added.

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=349ec8d908&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| “Last week, President Trump awarded Sen. Orrin Hatch, the longest-serving Senate Republican ever, the Medal of Freedom. In doing so, Mr. Trump emphasized the high personal regard in which he holds Mr. Hatch, but the medal was deserved not just because the president likes the senator, but because Orrin Hatch deserves recognition as a Senate great at the end of a distinguished career,” David Keene writes in _[The Washington Times](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=a1c691a7d6&e=3ee4985eea)_.
---
|
---
| “Mexico’s ambassador to Washington [Geronimo Gutierrez] has said that ‘there are people that have criminal backgrounds’ in the migrant caravan, lending weight to President Trump's assertion that it presents a threat to the United States,” Nicholas Ballasy reports in the _[Washington Examiner](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=f576299546&e=3ee4985eea)_. “A Department of Homeland Security official reportedly said on Monday the department estimates that there are more than 500 criminals traveling in the caravan heading toward the U.S.”
---
|
---
| "There’s a lot to be thankful for over the last two years. Of course you’d never know it if you turned on the TV,” Lauren DeBellis Appell writes in [Fox News](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=7a6935a049&e=3ee4985eea). “During the campaign, Trump vowed to protect religious liberty, and uphold the rule of law by nominating judges who would interpret the Constitution as written, not rewrite it according to their political whims. He did not disappoint."
---
|

[Privacy Policy](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=ecb87be7af&e=3ee4985eea) | [Contact the White House](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8dbdb2255f&e=3ee4985eea) | [Unsubscribe](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8cafb01033&e=3ee4985eea&c=9eaddc1216)
---
| | | The White House * 1600 Pennsylvania Ave NW * Washington, DC 20500 * USA · 202-456-1111
---

name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: Happy Thanksgiving to all!
body:
It's that time of year again, as many Americans travel far and wide to be with loved ones over the Thanksgiving holiday.

https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=71ff77c45f&e=3ee4985eea
The White House • November 21, 2018

** President Trump’s Thanksgiving message to Americans
------------------------------------------------------------
It's that time of year again, as many Americans travel far and wide to be with loved ones over the Thanksgiving holiday. Gathering in places of worship and around family dinner tables, it is a time to give appreciation for all that we've been blessed with.

"On Thanksgiving Day, we recall the courageous and inspiring journey of the Pilgrims who, nearly four centuries ago, ventured across the vast ocean to flee religious persecution and establish a home in the New World," President Donald J. Trump wrote in his 2018 Thanksgiving Proclamation (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=5709189e66&e=3ee4985eea) . Facing dangerous conditions and uncertainty, the more than 100 Pilgrims arrived at Plymouth, Massachusetts, and "instilled in our Nation a strong faith in God that continues to be a beacon of hope to all Americans."

In recognition of their journey, President George Washington issued the first Thanksgiving Proclamation on November 26, 1789. But it was under President Abraham Lincoln that the day became a true national celebration on the final Thursday of November each year.

"We give thanks for the family, friends, neighbors and loved ones who enrich our lives, lift our spirits, and fill our days with joy. And we give thanks to God, who continues to shed his almighty grace upon this magnificent land that we all love so very much," President Trump said yesterday from the Rose Garden.

"We are truly blessed to be Americans."

Thanksgiving at the White House, then and now (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=7d02e81792&e=3ee4985eea)

Watch: President Trump pardons this year’s National Thanksgiving Turkey! (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=7190f70586&e=3ee4985eea)

** The White House Christmas Tree has arrived!
------------------------------------------------------------
On Monday, the President joined First Lady Melania Trump as this year's White House Christmas Tree arrived by horse carriage to the North Portico.

"We received the @WhiteHouse Christmas tree yesterday! It will grace the Blue Room throughout the season – hope you will come to the People’s house to see it!" the First Lady shared on Twitter this week.

Each year, standing between 18 and 19 feet tall, the tree serves as the centerpiece for the White House Christmas decorations. By tradition, the winning tree farmer is determined from the results of the highly competitive National Christmas Tree Contest. This year's great honor goes to Larry Smith of Mountain Top Fraser Fir in Newland, North Carolina.

"It's like winning the Super Bowl," Smith said.

Watch the arrival of the 2018 White House Christmas Tree (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=b116c6a351&e=3ee4985eea) .

In photos: See more of this year’s Christmas tree arrival and setup (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=da24b1b7d5&e=3ee4985eea)

** Photo of the Day
------------------------------------------------------------
Official White House Photo by Andrea Hanks
President Donald J. Trump and First Lady Melania Trump receive the White House Christmas Tree | November 19, 2018

Privacy Policy (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=9e005cea48&e=3ee4985eea) | Contact the White House (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=94bbae94f2&e=3ee4985eea) | Unsubscribe (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8cafb01033&e=3ee4985eea&c=b039d7ce2a)

The White House • November 21, 2018 President Trump’s Thanksgiving message to Americans It's that time of year again, as many Americans travel far and wide to be with loved ones over the Thanksgiving holiday. Gathering in places of worship and around family dinner tables, it is a time to give appreciation for all that we've been blessed with. "On Thanksgiving Day, we recall the courageous and inspiring journey of the Pilgrims who, nearly four centuries ago, ventured across the vast ocean to flee religious persecution and establish a home in the New World," President Donald J. Trump wrote in his 2018 Thanksgiving Proclamation () . Facing dangerous conditions and uncertainty, the more than 100 Pilgrims arrived at Plymouth, Massachusetts, and "instilled in our Nation a strong faith in God that continues to be a beacon of hope to all Americans." In recognition of their journey, President George Washington issued the first Thanksgiving Proclamation on November 26, 1789. But it was under President Abraham Lincoln that the day became a true national celebration on the final Thursday of November each year. "We give thanks for the family, friends, neighbors and loved ones who enrich our lives, lift our spirits, and fill our days with joy. And we give thanks to God, who continues to shed his almighty grace upon this magnificent land that we all love so very much," President Trump said yesterday from the Rose Garden. "We are truly blessed to be Americans." Thanksgiving at the White House, then and now () Watch: President Trump pardons this year’s National Thanksgiving Turkey! () The White House Christmas Tree has arrived! On Monday, the President joined First Lady Melania Trump as this year's White House Christmas Tree arrived by horse carriage to the North Portico. "We received the @WhiteHouse Christmas tree yesterday! It will grace the Blue Room throughout the season – hope you will come to the People’s house to see it!" the First Lady shared on Twitter this week. Each year, standing between 18 and 19 feet tall, the tree serves as the centerpiece for the White House Christmas decorations. By tradition, the winning tree farmer is determined from the results of the highly competitive National Christmas Tree Contest. This year's great honor goes to Larry Smith of Mountain Top Fraser Fir in Newland, North Carolina. "It's like winning the Super Bowl," Smith said. Watch the arrival of the 2018 White House Christmas Tree () .
